(Italian pronunciation: [[ˈkjaːvari]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Italian "Help:IPA/Italian"); [Ligurian](/wiki/Ligurian_language "Ligurian language"): *Ciävai* [[ˈtʃaːvaj]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Ligurian "Help:IPA/Ligurian")) is a seaside town and [municipality](/wiki/Comune "Comune") in the [Metropolitan City of Genoa](/wiki/Metropolitan_City_of_Genoa "Metropolitan City of Genoa") in the region of [Liguria](/wiki/Liguria "Liguria") in [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy").[[3]](#cite_note-EB1911-3) As of 2025, with a population of 27,442, it is the [3rd-largest municipality in the metropolitan city](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_of_the_Metropolitan_City_of_Genoa "List of municipalities of the Metropolitan City of Genoa") and the [7th-largest in Liguria](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_of_Liguria "List of municipalities of Liguria").[[2]](#cite_note-population-2)
["Superficie di Comuni Province e Regioni italiane al 9 ottobre 2011"](https://www.istat.it/it/archivio/15622

**Chiavari** (Italian pronunciation: [[ˈkjaːvari]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Italian "Help:IPA/Italian"); [Ligurian](/wiki/Ligurian_language "Ligurian language"): *Ciävai* [[ˈtʃaːvaj]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Ligurian "Help:IPA/Ligurian")) is a seaside town and [municipality](/wiki/Comune "Comune") in the [Metropolitan City of Genoa](/wiki/Metropolitan_City_of_Genoa "Metropolitan City of Genoa") in the region of [Liguria](/wiki/Liguria "Liguria") in [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy").[[3]](#cite_note-EB1911-3) As of 2025, with a population of 27,442, it is the [3rd-largest municipality in the metropolitan city](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_of_the_Metropolitan_City_of_Genoa "List of municipalities of the Metropolitan City of Genoa") and the [7th-largest in Liguria](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_of_Liguria "List of municipalities of Liguria").[[2]](#cite_note-population-2)

It has a beachside promenade and a marina and is situated near the river [Entella](/wiki/Entella_(river) "Entella (river)").[[3]](#cite_note-EB1911-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiavari&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fc/Chiavari_castle.jpg/250px-Chiavari_castle.jpg)](/wiki/File:Chiavari_castle.jpg)

Chiavari Castle

### Pre-Roman and Roman Era

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiavari&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Pre-Roman and Roman Era")]

A pre-Roman necropolis, which dates from the 8th to 7th century BC, has been uncovered in the area where Chiavari is located now.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
Chiavari grew up on the traces of a Roman camp on the [Via Aurelia](/wiki/Via_Aurelia "Via Aurelia").

### Medieval Era

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiavari&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Medieval Era")]

A [castle](/wiki/Chiavari_Castle "Chiavari Castle") was constructed in 1147.[[3]](#cite_note-EB1911-3) The old town contains numerous arcades and buildings from the 13th century, including a castle, several mansions, and the nearby Church of San Salvatore di Lavagna, which was founded in 1224 by [Innocent IV](/wiki/Innocent_IV "Innocent IV").

The cathedral was rebuilt in 1613. Famous as a center of humanism, Chiavari has a public library with a collection of manuscripts and [incunabula](/wiki/Incunabula "Incunabula"). After the discovery of the conspiracy of the [Fieschi](/wiki/Fieschi "Fieschi"), in 1542, and the capture of Chiavari by the Counts of [Lavagna](/wiki/Lavagna "Lavagna"), the town suffered greatly, being associated with the conspirators. Among its illustrious citizens were Luca Cantiano di Moneglia, founder of an art academy, and Giuseppe Gregorio Solari, translator of many Latin poets.

### 19th century

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiavari&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 19th century")]

From 1805 to 1814, Chiavari served as the capital of the short-lived Apennins *Departments of France* of the [First French Empire](/wiki/First_French_Empire "First French Empire"). Chiavari is the home of the [Chiavari chair](/wiki/Chiavari_chair "Chiavari chair") designed in 1807 by a local, Giuseppe Gaetano Descalzi. The chair was a success and led to the opening of many factories in Chiavari and surrounding towns.

[Chiavari railway station](/wiki/Chiavari_railway_station "Chiavari railway station") was opened in 1868; it provides an underground passageway between the town centre and the beachside promenades.

### 20th century

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiavari&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 20th century")]

The Colonia Fara building was built in 1935 during the [Fascist regime](/wiki/Fascist_Italy_(1922%E2%80%931943) "Fascist Italy (1922–1943)").

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiavari&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Demographics")]

As of 2025, Chiavari has a population of 27,442, of whom 46.4% are male and 53.6% are female. Minors make up 13.3% of the population, and seniors make up 30.3%, compared to the Italian average of 14.9% and 24.7% respectively.[[2]](#cite_note-population-2)

### Foreign population

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiavari&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Foreign population")]

As of 2024, the foreign-born population is 3,733, equal to 13.6% of the population. The 5 largest foreign nationalities are [Albanians](/wiki/Albanians "Albanians") (768), [Ecuadorians](/wiki/Ecuadorians "Ecuadorians") (351), [Peruvians](/wiki/Peruvians "Peruvians") (322), [Ukrainians](/wiki/Ukrainians "Ukrainians") (266) and [Moroccans](/wiki/Moroccans "Moroccans") (200).[[9]](#cite_note-foreignborn-9)

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiavari&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Sports")]

In 2014, the local football team [Virtus Entella](/wiki/Virtus_Entella "Virtus Entella") were promoted to the Italian [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B") for the first time in their history. They were relegated back to the third tier in 2018 and in 2024 come back in Serie B
